H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H‑OS‑L1 (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) delivers the highest HEALING mean; 66 % positivity and a right‑skewed tail toward high scores. Qualitative responses (n=29) highlight rapid 10‑min settling and strong music approval. YouTube reach (30 views) is the strongest in the set, though retention metrics are unavailable. AutoGluon predicts an overstimulation score of 6.86, modestly below the observed mean, suggesting the model underestimates efficacy.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Strong calming effect for overstimulation, though few people have discovered it yet.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H‑OS‑L2 (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) shows a solid mean with 65 % positivity. Qualitative feedback (n=21) reports noticeable improvement and varied settle times (5‑10 min). YouTube views (5) are low. AutoGluon predicts a comparable overstimulation score (~6.86), aligning closely with observed performance.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Good relief from overstimulation, with most people feeling better in a few minutes.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H‑OS‑L3 (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) maintains a consistent profile; 64 % positivity and balanced distribution. Qualitative data (n=23) notes improvement within 5‑10 min and mixed music opinions. YouTube reach (8) is minimal. AutoGluon’s predicted overstimulation score (6.86) is slightly lower than observed, indicating a modest under‑prediction.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Consistent calming benefits, with listeners reporting improvement in under ten minutes.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H‑AX‑L1 (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) exhibits a modest right‑skew with 63 % positive responses. Qualitative data (n=23) reports noticeable improvement within ~10 min and balanced rhythm ratings. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views) with no retention data, indicating limited discovery. AutoGluon predicts an anxiety‑zone score of 7.84, modestly above the observed mean, suggesting room for optimization. Adviser sourcing is low (5/67), flagging a potential routing improvement.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners felt a noticeable calm within about ten minutes and liked the music.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H‑AX‑L2 (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) shows a tighter distribution and 67 % positivity. Qualitative feedback (n=20) notes rapid 5‑min settling for many listeners. Adviser influence is negligible (2/48), hinting at an adviser‑routing gap. YouTube views (6) remain low. AutoGluon assigns a predicted anxiety score of 7.84, higher than observed, indicating a possible design‑performance mismatch.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners reported moderate relief quickly, though few used the adviser feature.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H‑AX‑L3 (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) maintains a solid mean with a slightly right‑skewed distribution. Qualitative responses (n=20) emphasize noticeable improvement and 10‑min settle times. Adviser contribution is minimal (2/36). YouTube reach is very low (4 views). AutoGluon predicts an anxiety score of 7.84, again exceeding the observed mean, suggesting the protocol may be under‑captured by the model.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Generally positive responses with improvement noted after ten minutes.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H‑CR‑L1 (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) – see above entry for crisis protocol details.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In crisis moments users reported modest help, often feeling better within five minutes.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H‑CR‑L2 (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) – see above entry for crisis protocol details.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Slightly better relief for distressed listeners, with quick settling for many.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H‑CR‑L3 (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) – see above entry for crisis protocol details.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed results in crisis settings; some felt no change.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H‑SL‑L1 (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) yields a high mean with 65 % positivity; distribution leans right. Qualitative responses (n=15) emphasize 5‑10 min settling and strong music liking. YouTube views (4) are scarce. AutoGluon predicts a sleep‑zone score of 7.54, closely matching the observed mean.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Helps many fall asleep or rest, typically within five to ten minutes.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H‑SL‑L2 (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) shows a tight distribution and 67 % positivity. Qualitative data (n=15) reports improvement within ~10 min, though some listeners note neutrality toward music. YouTube views (5) remain low. AutoGluon’s sleep prediction (7.54) is marginally below the observed mean.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Effective for sleep, with most listeners noticing improvement after about ten minutes.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H‑SL‑L3 (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) sustains a solid mean with 64 % positivity. Qualitative feedback (n=22) highlights quick settling (5‑10 min) and balanced rhythm assessments. YouTube reach (3) is minimal. AutoGluon’s sleep prediction (7.54) aligns well with the observed outcome.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Provides a calming sleep aid that settles quickly for most users.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T‑FC‑L1 (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) underperforms relative to THRIVING focus mean; 56 % positivity. Qualitative feedback (n=23) shows mixed improvement and some “slightly busy” rhythm reports. YouTube views (5) are low. AutoGluon predicts a focus score of 7.73, higher than observed, indicating a prediction‑performance gap.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus benefits are modest; some listeners felt the rhythm was a bit busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T‑FC‑L2 (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) demonstrates strong focus outcomes with 66 % positivity and balanced distribution. Qualitative responses (n=17) note noticeable improvement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views (4) remain scarce. AutoGluon predicts a focus score of 7.73, closely matching observed performance.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Good focus aid, with most listeners reporting noticeable improvement.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T‑FC‑L3 (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) attains the highest focus mean; 71 % positivity despite a wide spread. Qualitative data (n=29) reports significant improvement and ~10 min settle times, though some rhythm comments note slight busyness. YouTube reach (15) is the strongest in THRIVING. AutoGluon predicts a focus score of 7.73, aligning with the high observed mean.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Very effective for focus, though reactions to the rhythm vary slightly.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T‑CF‑L1 (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) – see above entry for creative‑flow details.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Slight creative boost that some found only a modest improvement.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T‑CF‑L2 (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) – see above entry for creative‑flow details.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: A modest lift in creative flow, with a few listeners feeling better after a short time.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T‑CF‑L3 (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) – see above entry for creative‑flow details.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Strong creative uplift, with many noting significant improvement.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T‑MB‑L1 (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) yields solid maintenance scores with 64 % positivity. Qualitative feedback (n=23) notes longer settle times (~20 min) during therapy sessions. YouTube views (3) are minimal. AutoGluon predicts a maintenance score of 7.69, modestly above observed, suggesting potential under‑performance.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Steady maintenance and balance, though some need longer time to feel the effect.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T‑MB‑L2 (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) shows higher variance and lower mean (59 % positivity). Qualitative responses (n=16) include mixed improvement and some “no effect” reports. YouTube views (4) are low. AutoGluon’s maintenance prediction (7.69) exceeds observed mean, indicating a discrepancy.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed balance results; a few listeners didn’t notice much change.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T‑MB‑L3 (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) maintains a reasonable mean with 71 % positivity despite small n. Qualitative data (n=19) reports quick settling (~5 min) and balanced rhythm. YouTube reach (3) is low. AutoGluon predicts a maintenance score of 7.69, aligning above the observed mean.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Promising balance aid, with quick settling for many despite the small sample.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X‑ME‑L1 (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) yields a moderate mood‑energy score; 54 % positivity. Qualitative responses (n=22) note moderate improvement and occasional delayed settling (up to 20 min). YouTube views (8) are low. AutoGluon predicts a low‑affect zone score of 8.23, substantially higher than observed, indicating latent potential.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Moderate mood lift; many liked the music but overall impact was modest.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X‑ME‑L2 (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) improves slightly; 64 % positivity and quick 5‑10 min settling. Qualitative feedback (n=15) reports strong music liking. YouTube reach (8) remains modest. AutoGluon’s low‑affect prediction (8.23) exceeds the observed mean.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Good mood boost with quick settling for most listeners.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X‑ME‑L3 (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) shows the lowest mood‑energy mean; 56 % positivity. Qualitative data (n=25) includes mixed improvement reports and fast settling for many. YouTube views (4) are minimal. AutoGluon predicts a low‑affect score of 8.23, higher than observed, suggesting a possible unmet design potential.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed mood effects; some felt better, others saw little change.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X‑MD‑L1 (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) shows low motivation scores; 45 % positivity. Qualitative feedback (n=17) is mixed, with quick settling for some but limited impact for others. YouTube reach (5) is scarce. AutoGluon predicts a motivation score of 7.88, far above the observed mean, indicating a strong model‑data divergence.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation boost is low overall; some listeners felt little effect.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X‑MD‑L2 (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) records the lowest motivation mean; 43 % positivity. Qualitative responses (n=21) note modest improvement and longer settle times. YouTube views (7) are low. AutoGluon’s motivation prediction (7.88) substantially exceeds observed performance.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Limited motivation improvement, with many noticing only modest changes.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X‑MD‑L3 (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) stands out with a high mean despite small n; 62 % positivity and pronounced variance, suggesting possible bimodality. Qualitative data (n=17) reports moderate to significant improvement but mixed rhythm opinions. YouTube reach (7) is limited. AutoGluon predicts a motivation score of 7.88, aligning with the high observed mean but underscoring variance concerns.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Promising motivation gains for some, though the experience is inconsistent.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X‑MC‑L1 (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) records moderate mind‑clarity outcomes; 54 % positivity. Qualitative feedback (n=25) includes both improvement and two reports of worsening effects. YouTube views (8) are limited. AutoGluon predicts a mind‑clarity score of 7.54, substantially higher than observed, suggesting a possible population split.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mixed reactions for mental clarity; most liked the music but a few felt worse.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X‑MC‑L2 (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) shows a slight improvement; 56 % positivity. Qualitative data (n=20) reports very calming rhythms and moderate improvement. YouTube reach (12) remains modest. AutoGluon’s predicted mind‑clarity score (7.54) exceeds the observed mean, hinting at latent efficacy.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Generally calming and helpful for clarity, with most listeners noting improvement.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X‑MC‑L3 (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) registers the lowest mind‑clarity mean with high dispersion; 50 % positivity. Qualitative responses (n=17) are mixed, with some noting no change or worsening. YouTube views (8) are low. AutoGluon predicts a mind‑clarity score of 7.54, markedly higher than observed, flagging a potential bimodal subgroup effect.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Varied outcomes for clarity; many felt little change or even a decline.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Across the three series the data suggests overall stable efficacy with HEALING showing the most consistent outcomes, THRIVING delivering strong peaks in focus, and TRANSFORMING displaying the widest variance, as expected for its ascending‑arc designs.
• HEALING has the largest sample (n=574) and lowest std, indicating reliable performance across entry points.
• THRIVING matches HEALING in mean but with fewer responses, highlighting a few standout protocols (e.g., T‑FC‑L3).
• TRANSFORMING’s higher std and lower mean point to heterogeneous listener responses and possible subgroup effects.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Design intent appears to shape response profiles, with each series showing characteristic patterns.
• HEALING protocols achieve steady DESCENT efficacy and modest variance across anxiety, overstimulation, and sleep contexts.
• THRIVING protocols, especially HOLD designs, produce high peaks for Focus & Clarity and Creative Flow, though some show modest engagement.
• TRANSFORMING protocols exhibit the greatest spread, reflecting the challenge of ASCENT arcs for low‑affect states and suggesting population splits.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols stand out as statistical outliers or display unexpected model‑prediction mismatches.
• X‑MD‑L3 shows unusually high variance (std=2.38) with a mean that exceeds many peers, hinting at bimodal subgroup effects.
• X‑MC‑L3 records a low mean (5.70) and high std (2.45), possibly indicating a population mismatch.
• H‑CR‑L3 contains a small but notable fraction of “No effect” responses despite crisis‑design expectations.
These signals suggest targeted subgroup or design investigations.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Several convergent opportunities emerge for protocol refinement and further data collection.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑OS‑L1 (n=95) – strong efficacy but low YouTube discovery; action: enhance metadata and cross‑promotion.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MD‑L3 (n=13) – high variance yet promising mean; action: develop a Level 2 variant with extended ASCENT phase to test consistency.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MC‑L3 (n=10) – low mean with mixed feedback; action: probe population split and consider a tailored L2 variant for low‑motivation users.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑AX‑L1 (n=67) – cross‑bucket routing and low adviser usage; action: adjust Adviser routing to capture anxiety and low‑affect listeners.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑FC‑L3 (n=35) – top focus performance; action: increase hold phase duration to explore further gains.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑CR‑L1 (n=43) – site‑driven traffic suggests adviser routing optimization could improve crisis outcomes.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift is prevalent, reflecting evolving data patterns across runs, while semantic clustering remains under‑powered.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 27 protocols show notable drift (similarity <0.85), especially many TRANSFORMING entries.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: insufficient embeddings to form meaningful clusters.
• Confidence note: drift metrics are limited by the short historical window and sparse embedding coverage.

by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Strong calming effect for overstimulation, though few people have discovered it yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Good relief from overstimulation, with most people feeling better in a few minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Consistent calming benefits, with listeners reporting improvement in under ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Helps many fall asleep or rest, typically within five to ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Effective for sleep, with most listeners noticing improvement after about ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Provides a calming sleep aid that settles quickly for most users.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Slight creative boost that some found only a modest improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: A modest lift in creative flow, with a few listeners feeling better after a short time.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Strong creative uplift, with many noting significant improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus benefits are modest; some listeners felt the rhythm was a bit busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Good focus aid, with most listeners reporting noticeable improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Very effective for focus, though reactions to the rhythm vary slightly.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Steady maintenance and balance, though some need longer time to feel the effect.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Mixed balance results; a few listeners didn’t notice much change.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Promising balance aid, with quick settling for many despite the small sample.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mixed reactions for mental clarity; most liked the music but a few felt worse.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Generally calming and helpful for clarity, with most listeners noting improvement.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Varied outcomes for clarity; many felt little change or even a decline.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation boost is low overall; some listeners felt little effect.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Limited motivation improvement, with many noticing only modest changes.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Promising motivation gains for some, though the experience is inconsistent.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Moderate mood lift; many liked the music but overall impact was modest.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Good mood boost with quick settling for most listeners.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Mixed mood effects; some felt better, others saw little change.
